markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Map WP-009 concepts to current modules: system architecture, local-first infrastructure, LLM infrastructure, product surfaces, OSS foundations, and appendices. This MT belongs to MasterSpecEnrichmentSeed. Prepare the future Master Spec refinement/enrichment payload without relying on sidecar research.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Prepare spec-enrichment-ready wording without editing the Master Spec in this stub task.
Where current Master Spec text is stale, mark the exact stale contract and the replacement Handshake-native rule.
scope.expected_behavior: SpecModuleAnchorMap leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: SpecModuleAnchorMap is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Anchor map for modules 02/07/10/11/12 recorded in refinement.json spec_anchors and applied in v02.192 (content-verified twice: evidence pass and adversarial review pass both opened the cited sections; see lifecycle.adversarial_review.refutation_attempts).

03-local-first-infrastructure.md disposition: NO_ANCHOR_REQUIRED - grep 'WP-KERNEL-009|ProjectKnowledgeIndex|KnowledgeClaim|RichDocument|UserManualRecord' = 0 matches (2026-06-11); local-first storage law for WP-009 is carried by 02#2.3.13.11 PostgreSQL/EventLedger clauses.

04-llm-infrastructure.md disposition: NO_ANCHOR_REQUIRED - same grep 0 matches; WP-009 retrieval/memory law carried by 02#2.3.13.11 (MemoryPassage/RetrievalTrace) + 2.3.14 AI-Ready Data; module 04 model-runtime sections owned by other packets.

AC2 (exact files/state named): scope.module_anchor_disposition enumerates all seven v02.192 spec modules with ANCHORED section ids or NO_ANCHOR_REQUIRED dispositions backed by a named, reproducible grep (pattern and date recorded).
AC3 (no sidecar authority): the disposition is recorded in this typed MT contract; the anchors themselves live in the applied v02.192 bundle resolved through SPEC_CURRENT.md.
AC4 (HBR rows): this MT's hbr_focus rows are record-scope only; disposition NA per packet acceptance_matrix.hbr_not_applicable HBRNA-002 (MasterSpecEnrichmentSeed group).

concerns: Contract freshness skew: scope.relevant_files and the activation constraint pin the v02.191 bundle while SPEC_CURRENT and all evidence are v02.192 (contracts hydrated 07:20Z after the 06:45Z spec bump); validator should read the constraint intent (indexed bundle, not v02.182 monolith), but a hygiene pass updating contract pins to v02.192 would remove the ambiguity.

AC coverage: evidence addresses AC1 only; AC2 (state tables/failure modes/verification evidence), AC3 (sidecar rejection), and AC4 (HBR disposition - hbr_focus here includes CX-503R) have no evidence entries, unlike sibling MTs MT-034..048 which carry explicit AC2/AC3/AC4 rows.

Unverifiable agent citation: EV1 says "verified by evidence-mapper agent" - no receipt or artifact for that agent run exists; the underlying claims were independently re-verified by this review, but the citation itself proves nothing.

Coverage note (scope-conformant but worth validator attention): the disposition covers modules 02/07/10/11/12/03/04 only; 05-security-and-observability has no disposition row even though WP-009 carries secrets/permissions scope (the MT scope text does not list module 05, so this is not a contract breach).
adversarial_review.refutation_attempts: Opened every cited artifact (refinement.json, spec-enrichment-proposal.json, stub contract, v02.192 spec modules 02/07/10/11/12, spec-changelog.jsonl, SPEC_CURRENT.md, packet.json); attacked criterion coverage AC1-AC4, citation validity, specificity, v02.192 freshness, and scope-artifact existence. Re-ran the disposition grep myself: grep -E "WP-KERNEL-009|ProjectKnowledgeIndex|KnowledgeClaim|RichDocument|UserManualRecord" on v02.192 modules 03 and 04 returned 0 matches for both, reproducing the recorded claim; verified refinement.spec_anchors and all six anchored headings on disk. Fix: add AC2/AC3/AC4 evidence entries (mirror the MT-034 template) and either drop or substantiate the evidence-mapper citation.

items: Replaced unverifiable agent citation with reproducible verification facts (adversarial reviewer independently reproduced the 0-match greps on modules 03/04).
Note: module 05-security-and-observability was not in this MT's declared scope; flagged to external validator via adversarial_review concerns.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
